decision.archive_summary: The GC/minor-GC regression work appears resolved. Earlier steps added fixed live eval coverage for GC-D2 and a harder natural-Chinese multi-topic return case, then tightened the packaged guard so it does not override a shadow-guard veto. The user also expressed an enduring preference to avoid whitelist-style or hardcoded handling and to include harder natural Chinese multi-turn topic-switch cases in fixed regression coverage. Later turns narrowed to whether the previously failing test should now pass, followed by a request to rerun automatically; the assistant reported it had already rerun the live eval command.

The latest user request was satisfied by the assistant, so earlier raw turns are good eviction candidates. I pinned the user’s anti-whitelist preference and the resulting implementation summary because those are the most reusable semantic facts if the topic resurfaces; the rest is solved status history.
